action.hypothesis: Recent new families have all clustered far above the current hybrid full frontier, so the most credible next move is a training-dynamics pass on the one family that has actually improved full FVU. Adding a modest orthogonality penalty to hybrid_topk may reduce decoder interference between active features, sharpen the effective support at K=128, and improve reconstruction quality without changing the proven architecture.
action.summary: Adaptive-threshold TopK matched the jumprelu-level proxy plateau rather than breaking through, so architecture exploration has not yet produced a stronger mainline candidate. This round returns to the best-known hybrid_topk K=128/ef=8/Adam setup and tests a single quality-focused regularization change, using proxy first to see whether reduced decoder interference can move FVU meaningfully below the recent 0.27 proxy band.
action.self_review: This is a narrow follow-up on the only family with a verified full win, not another broad sweep. The risk is that orthogonality regularization could trade away too much fitting capacity, but it is a clean way to test whether the remaining bottleneck is feature interference rather than encoder expressivity.
notes_to_memory: Adaptive_threshold_topk did not improve beyond the recent jumprelu-level proxy plateau, so support-suppression alone is not enough to beat the current hybrid mainline.
Round 15 returns to hybrid_topk for a training-dynamics probe, testing whether mild decoder orthogonality regularization can improve K=128 quality by reducing active-feature interference.
next_hypotheses: If hybrid_topk with ortho regularization improves proxy FVU materially, promote it to full as the next mainline quality test.
If the ortho pass is flat or worse, bias the next round toward a more expressive new architecture edit rather than more local regularization sweeps.
